claim: The Schwarzschild vacuum metric has Ricci scalar R = 0 outside r = 2M.
success_criteria: Defines the Schwarzschild metric or an equivalent static spherically symmetric metric with f(r)=1-2M/r.
Computes Ricci scalar, Ricci tensor contraction, or an equivalent curvature residual.
Shows the residual simplifies to zero under r > 2M and M > 0 assumptions.
failure_modes: Only states the known result without computing a curvature residual.
Confuses Ricci scalar with Kretschmann scalar.
Evaluates at a single numeric point and treats that as a symbolic proof.
prompt: Test whether a static spherically symmetric Schwarzschild metric with f(r)=1-2M/r has vanishing Ricci scalar outside r=2M. Produce a symbolic residual that can validate or refute the claim.
